title: Battery Thermal Containment and Recovery
content: Trigger conditions: battery_temp_c above threshold for two consecutive windows or a single critical spike above emergency cap. Immediate actions: reduce drive duty cycle by 30 percent, disable non-critical compute workloads, and command fan profile to high. Safety checks: verify no battery enclosure deformation, check for odor or smoke, and confirm thermal sensor continuity. Diagnostic sequence: inspect intake and exhaust duct paths, compare fan command versus RPM telemetry, and confirm pack-level cell delta stays below service limit. Recovery criteria: temperature trend decreases for three windows, fan response remains stable, and no new thermal alerts are raised. Escalation: if trend does not decrease within 10 minutes, remove robot from service and schedule battery bay teardown. Post-incident notes: attach before-and-after thermal plots, operator observations, and cleaned component list.

title: Motor Current Fault Isolation
content: Trigger conditions: motor_current_a sustained above threshold during standard payload profile. Immediate actions: cap acceleration profile, move to low-load lane, and pause non-essential tasks. Inspection path: validate payload mass against route manifest, inspect wheel drag and gearbox friction, and check actuator alignment markers. Electrical validation: verify phase balance, inspect motor harness connectors, and compare commanded torque versus measured current. Mechanical validation: rotate drive train manually where safe, inspect belts and couplings for binding, and confirm no foreign object in caster assembly. Decision points: if electrical checks pass but drag persists, classify as mechanical resistance; if phase imbalance persists, classify as motor controller or harness issue. Recovery criteria: current trend returns to baseline across two full route cycles.

title: Network Latency Wave Response
content: Trigger conditions: network_latency_ms and packet retransmits exceed baseline across multiple robots in one zone. Containment actions: throttle non-critical telemetry, pin active robots to nearest healthy access point, and reduce map streaming frequency. Infrastructure checks: inspect AP client count, channel utilization, roaming events, switch port errors, and uplink packet drops. Fleet checks: identify robots with highest retry rate, compare signal strength by corridor, and isolate if issue correlates with one AP cluster. Mitigation sequence: rebalance channels, disable noisy background sync jobs, and reroute robots through known healthy corridors. Recovery criteria: latency returns below service threshold for 15 minutes and retransmit rate normalizes. Escalation path: if uplink degradation persists, isolate zone and dispatch network operations for hardware replacement.

title: CPU Thermal Throttle Procedure
content: Trigger conditions: cpu_temp_c near thermal throttle point or repeated thermal throttling events in under one hour. Immediate response: suspend non-critical inference jobs, preserve safety monitoring loops, and increase cooling duty cycle. Workload triage: identify top CPU processes, stop debug replays and heavy exports, and stagger compute tasks. Hardware checks: inspect heatsink seating torque, confirm fan speed telemetry, and clear dust from intake paths. Thermal integrity checks: compare onboard temperature sensors and external probe sample where available. Recovery criteria: CPU temperature stabilizes below warning threshold during representative workload for two windows. Escalation: if throttle recurs after software load shedding, schedule hardware service and replace thermal interface material.

title: Disk IO Latency Mitigation
content: Trigger conditions: disk_io_wait_ms above threshold with delayed telemetry writes or event backlog growth. Immediate actions: pause verbose trace exports, rotate oversized log files, and verify available disk headroom. Inspection checklist: check filesystem fill level, identify top write amplifiers, and inspect container retention policies. Integrity checks: confirm no fs corruption alerts and review SMART indicators for media degradation. Mitigation path: prune stale artifacts, compress non-critical historical logs, and adjust log level to production baseline. Recovery criteria: io wait returns to baseline and write queue depth stabilizes across two cycles. Escalation: if IO wait remains elevated under low load, create hardware replacement ticket and migrate workload away from affected unit.

title: Sensor Drift Validation and Recalibration
content: Trigger conditions: sensor_drift_pct increases gradually or jumps after impact event. Immediate actions: reduce autonomy aggressiveness, switch to conservative navigation profile, and start reference validation routine. Validation sequence: compare against calibrated reference target, inspect sensor mount alignment, and verify connector seating and cable strain. Calibration sequence: run in-place calibration, store new baseline, and repeat validation pass to confirm consistency. Decision points: if drift persists after recalibration, classify as hardware misalignment or sensor aging. Recovery criteria: drift percentage remains within tolerance over two mission windows and no mismatch alarms fire. Escalation: route robot to maintenance bench for mount correction or sensor replacement.

title: Wheel Slip and Traction Recovery
content: Trigger conditions: wheel_slip_ratio above threshold on repeated segments or multiple robots in same corridor. Immediate actions: lower acceleration and turning aggressiveness, avoid suspect lanes, and notify floor operations. Environment checks: inspect floor for fluid, dust, or packaging debris and verify ramp surface condition. Hardware checks: inspect tire tread wear, wheel alignment, and load distribution. Fleet response: if more than three robots show correlated slip in same zone, isolate lane and reroute tasks. Recovery criteria: slip ratio returns to baseline across two route passes under normal payload. Escalation: if slip persists after floor remediation, schedule wheel replacement and suspension inspection.

title: Power Bus Voltage Sag Response
content: Trigger conditions: power_bus_v drops below operational lower bound during acceleration or charging transitions. Immediate actions: reduce propulsion demand, cancel rapid charge handoffs, and move robot to safe standby zone. Electrical checks: inspect connector tightness, look for heat discoloration at terminals, and validate battery contactor behavior. Charging checks: verify charger output stability, grounding, and cable integrity. Decision points: if sag appears only under load, inspect internal resistance trend and bus harness losses; if sag appears at idle, inspect sensing and control board health. Recovery criteria: bus voltage remains stable during controlled load profile. Escalation: remove unit from duty and open electrical safety inspection ticket.

title: Payload Imbalance Detection and Handling
content: Trigger conditions: repeated balance alarms, uneven motor load, or abnormal vibration during loaded transit. Immediate actions: reduce speed, route to nearest inspection waypoint, and pause new pickup tasks. Verification steps: compare manifest weight with measured load estimate, inspect tote placement, and check fixture lock engagement. Mechanical checks: inspect suspension response and frame leveling sensors. Corrective actions: rebalance payload, reseat fixture, and run a short validation route. Recovery criteria: balance indicators remain within tolerance and drive current symmetry normalizes. Escalation: if imbalance repeats with verified payloads, inspect frame alignment and suspension components.

title: Emergency Stop Root Cause Workflow
content: Trigger conditions: emergency stop activation without planned operator command. Immediate safety actions: maintain stop state, capture event and telemetry logs, and verify no injury or physical hazard. Control checks: inspect e-stop circuit continuity, check interlock relay state, and confirm controller fault flags. Environment checks: identify obstacles, collisions, or proximity sensor events before stop. Diagnostic branch: if hardware interlock anomaly is detected, lock out robot pending electrical inspection; if obstacle-induced stop is confirmed, validate sensor thresholds and map boundaries. Recovery criteria: root cause identified, corrective action documented, and supervised restart completed successfully.

title: Fleet Zone Isolation and Reroute
content: Trigger conditions: correlated anomalies across multiple robots in one physical zone such as latency, slip, or thermal alerts. Immediate actions: prevent new assignments into affected zone, reroute active tasks, and notify operators with scope and impact. Shared infrastructure checks: inspect access points, charging rails, conveyors, and local power distribution. Operational controls: reduce throughput target temporarily and prioritize safety-critical missions. Restoration sequence: clear root cause, validate with two canary robots, and re-enable assignments in stages. Recovery criteria: no repeat anomalies for 20 minutes at controlled load. Escalation: maintain isolation and open cross-functional incident bridge if anomalies recur.

title: Predictive Maintenance Escalation Policy
content: Trigger conditions: any major threshold crossed three times in seven days or two distinct subsystems trending toward failure on same unit. Immediate actions: classify unit as reduced-duty, attach incident history bundle, and schedule maintenance slot within SLA window. Review checklist: include recent telemetry trends, prior repairs, and unresolved warnings from verifier checks. Planning actions: pre-stage replacement parts, assign technician ownership, and define validation route after maintenance. Return-to-service criteria: no critical alerts, confidence score above acceptance floor, and successful supervised mission trial. Governance: require post-maintenance report with findings, replaced parts, and preventive recommendation updates.
